summary: DHH announces the relaunch of 37signals' workshop series, rebranded from 'Building of Basecamp' to 'The Getting Real Workshop.' The workshop covers the same core philosophy but draws from a broader range of examples across all 37signals applications. Capped at 50 attendees for an intimate format, it sold out in just 12 hours—14 times faster than the previous workshop.
key_points: 37signals rebranded their workshop from 'Building of Basecamp' to 'Getting Real,' broadening the scope beyond a single product
The 50-person cap is a deliberate choice to foster intimate discussion and audience interaction
Selling out in 12 hours (vs. one week previously) signals rapidly growing demand for 37signals' philosophy
money_quotes: The 50-person cap creates a intimate atmosphere with lots of great questions and interactions.
Sold out. All 50 seats where gone in 12 hours. That's about 14 times as fast as last time.
key_insight: The explosive demand for 37signals' Getting Real workshop reflected a growing appetite in the industry for their contrarian, lean approach to building software products.
